demographic_audit_note: Audited Atlantis record against facility face sheet; verified Straight Medicare eligibility. Address in Harston Hall confirmed, but primary phone number was discovered to be the patient's disconnected landline; updated Atlantis with daughter's (POA) mobile number.
home_vs_ltc_determination: SW initially indicated Home goal, but after a family conference on Day 12, the goal was pivoted to Long-Term Care (LTC) due to the patient's inability to perform ADLs safely following the MS flare.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: No, I haven't heard of you. Q2: To help me get stronger? Q3: The nurse said someone might come to the house. Q4: I am terrified of falling when I try to get to the bathroom at night—escalated this specific mobility anxiety to SW.
weekly_facility_update: Week 1: Patient making slow progress in PT; SW still eyeing a Friday discharge. Week 2: SW reports a 'Safety Pivot'; patient's MS flare has resulted in significant neurogenic bladder issues and the family has verbalized they cannot manage the catheter care at home.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into daughter's iPhone during initial intake; program flyer delivered to patient's bedside table.
hha_confirmation_status: Not In Place. Reason: LTC_Pivot_Abort. The Social Worker has officially shifted the discharge plan from Home Health to a permanent LTC placement within the facility's skilled wing.
stage_3_followup_audit: Audit not completed. Per the LTC Filter rule, all transition coordination was suspended once the SW confirmed the LTC goal.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Call not performed; patient is no longer a candidate for home transition services.
atlantis_final_sync: Logged Neutral_LTC_Closure in Atlantis on Day 14. No discharge date or first visit date entered as the patient is remaining in the facility.
ma_visit_booking: MA not scheduled. Scheduling an MA for a patient pivoting to LTC is a Banned Action and would violate the HHA-First Rule.
narrative_summary: During the ENTRY/TRIAGE stage, the Connection Intake revealed significant patient anxiety regarding home safety, which was immediately escalated to the SW. In the MAINTENANCE stage, weekly check-ins with the SW highlighted a decline in the patient's functional status and a lack of caregiver support for catheter management. Once the SW determined the goal was Long-Term Care (LTC), the LTC Filter was applied. A Neutral_LTC_Closure was performed in Atlantis to prevent overstepping into the SW's placement process.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Connection Intake Q4 surfaces profound fear of night-time falls.
Atlantis demographic audit reveals disconnected landline for primary contact.
Social Worker formalizes the pivot to Long-Term Care (LTC).

format_2_triples.situation: Patient expresses high anxiety about managing catheter care alone at home during Stage 1 meeting.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Documented responses and escalated Q4 concerns to the SW immediately.

format_2_triples.situation: Atlantis queue lists an outdated home phone number for the patient.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Cross-referenced face sheet and updated Atlantis with POA's mobile number.

format_2_triples.situation: SW informs PN that the patient will be staying in the facility for LTC.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Stopped all home care education and performed a Neutral_LTC_Closure in Atlantis.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: After the SW announces the LTC pivot, the PN steps back and stops attending the patient's weekly updates to allow the SW to manage the placement logistics.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW Boundary and the LTC Filter; once the goal is LTC, the PN's role in the transition ceases.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Upon hearing the family's concerns about catheter care, the PN documents the 'Caregiver Training Gap' and provides the SW with a summary of the patient's Q4 anxiety responses to assist the SW's safety assessment.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Follows the Connection Intake protocol and ensures critical barriers are escalated to the appropriate professional (the SW) without attempting to solve them.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN contacts the facility's billing department to provide a cost-analysis comparing the expense of 24/7 home health aides versus the LTC room rate to help the family decide.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Banned Action: Proving cost analysis of home care vs LTC is an automatic overstep and interferes with the SW's financial counseling role.
